business.headline: European gas futures jump 42% after Qatar halts LNG amid Middle East conflictEuropean natural gas prices have surged sharply due to the halt in LNG supplies caused by Middle East conflict. This disruption highlights Europe's growing energy vulnerability amid geopolitical tensions.

QatarEnergy to halt LPG production as Middle East tensions deepenThe crisis entered its third day as Iran widened its retaliation following joint US–Israeli strikes that killed its Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, along with several senior military and political figures.

If 50% of insurers offer mental health cover, why is usage below 20%?While it is encouraging that mental health cover is becoming more common, the low usage rate raises questions. Are employees fully aware of what is available to them? Do they know how to access these services? Or are they still hesitant to seek help?
EPFO's interest stays at 8.25% for FY26The last change in the rate came in 2024, when it was raised from 8.15% to 8.25% for 2023-24. Since then, there has been no further increase or reduction.
Sensex ends 1,048 lower, Nifty below 24,900; RIL down 2%The S&P BSE Sensex lost 1,048.34 points to close at 80,238.85, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 312.95 points to end at 24,865.70.

From LPG prices to UPI rules: 3 key March 2026 changes that could impact your budgetMarch 2026 has brought a few important changes that could affect your monthly budget. From LPG cylinder prices to new UPI rules and railway updates, here are three key changes you should know about.

Strait of Hormuz is buzzing again and why it matters to the global economyThe Strait of Hormuz is a narrow waterway between Iran and Oman that connects the Gulf to the Arabian Sea. On a typical day, ships carrying oil equal to about one-fifth of global demand pass through it.

Rs 9 lakh crore gone as brutal Dalal Street crash rattles investorsThe sell-off on Dalal Street has erased over Rs 9 lakh crore in investor wealth, dragging BSE's total market capitalisation down from nearly Rs 463 lakh crore to around Rs 454 lakh crore during intraday trade.

Bitcoin at $66,000 mark: 3 things crypto investors must know as Iran–US tensions riseBitcoin is hovering around the $66,000 level as global markets reel from rising Iran–US tensions. However, the key question remains: what's next?

Sensex nosedives 1,500 points today: Should investors be worried?Markets are sliding under Middle East tensions, surging crude, US–Iran hostilities and fears of trade blockages. Fundamentals look steady for now, but if the conflict deepens, is this only the first shock?

Gold jumps Rs 6,000, silver soars Rs 10,000: How Iran–US tensions affect your moneyGold and silver surged as Iran–US tensions drove investors towards safer assets. The big question is, what does this mean for your money?

RVNL, IRFC down up to 5%: Why are these railway stocks falling today?RVNL was down 5.74% to Rs 298.20 at around 12:03 pm, while IRFC shares were down 4.65% to Rs 98.74. The weakness in these names comes at a time when sentiment across domestic equities has turned cautious as global risk appetite faded and benchmark indices weakened.

L&T share price tumbles over 5%. Why is the stock falling today?L&T's slide highlights how surging crude and geopolitical uncertainty are unsettling infrastructure stocks, prompting investors to reassess cost risks and project visibility until conditions stabilise in conflict-hit regions.

Paras Defence shares jump 12%: Why are defence stocks rising today?Defence stocks across the board were firm even as the rest of the market struggled with weak sentiment linked to rising crude prices and the worsening conflict involving Iran and the United States.

Sensex tumbles 1,048 points amid Iran-US conflict: 6 things you need to knowThe S&P BSE Sensex fell sharply in early trade, and the NSE Nifty50 also slipped more than 2%, as investors reacted to the fast-changing situation between the US, Israel and Iran.

Sensex tanks 1,000 points as oil surges amid ongoing Iran-US conflictThe S&P BSE Sensex fell over 1,000 points in early trade, slipping below 81,000. The NSE Nifty50 dropped under 24,900. This was one of the steepest opening declines in recent weeks.

Will stock market fall today amid Iran's war with the US, Israel?Early signals from Gift Nifty suggest a weak start. Gift Nifty futures were trading at 25,60 points at 8:30 am. This indicates that the benchmark Nifty 50 may open below Friday's close of 25,178.65.

Sufficient stock, India won't face immediate oil disruption amid Iran crisisIndia imports 88 per cent of its crude oil – the raw material for making fuels like petrol and diesel – and about half of its requirement for natural gas - the feedstock for generating electricity, producing fertiliser, fuel for household kitchens and CNG for automobiles – and cooking gas LPG.

India built UPI and Aadhaar, is it time for an Aatmanirbhar credit score too?A three-digit number may look small on paper. But for millions of Indians, it quietly decides how far they can go. India built digital infrastructure for identity (AADHAAR) and payment (UPI). The next question is whether credit deserves the same clarity and control.

entertainment.headline: Watch: Allu Arjun, Nagarjuna, Atlee at Allu Sirish-Nayanika's pre-wedding bash Allu Sirish and Nayanika Reddy's pre-wedding party was attended by Allu Arjun, Nagarjuna, Atlee, Nandamuri Balakrishna and others. The couple will get married on March 6, 2026.

Vivek Oberoi, in Dubai, urges people not to spread panic amid Middle East tensions Vivek Oberoi, who is currently in Dubai, called for peace and unity amid tensions in the Middle East. He urged people not to spread panic in these sensitive times.

Ranbir Kapoor had a witty jibe for paps getting too close at event. Read it here Actor Ranbir Kapoor was in the mood for banter when a photographer tried to get too close for a shot at the Indian Super Gaming League.

Why Arijit Singh left Mumbai to return to hometown Jiaganj: Singer's father reveals Arijit Singh's father, Surinder Singh recalled their family's journey from Lahore to Jiaganj, West Bengal, after Partition. He opened up on why Arijit is always drawn back to his hometown.

Toxic song Tabaahi: Yash, Kiara's romantic track is out minus video, fans react The first audio single from Yash and Kiara Advani's upcoming film Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups has been released. The track, titled Tabaahi, is available in several languages, but the music video has not been unveiled. This has prompted disappointment among fans.

Nora Fatehi says she's safe in India, and Middle East visuals have shaken her Actor Nora Fatehi, who resides in Dubai, has addressed her followers amid the ongoing Middle East conflict, stating that she is currently safe in India. She called for global unity while expressing concern over recent violent developments.

Mandana Karimi calls Iranian regime cancerous, talks about Middle East crisis Actor Mandana Karimi has spoken about the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, sharing her personal distress and condemning the Iranian regime, which she called a 'cancerous cell' in the region.

Chris Hemsworth credits blunt honesty for lasting marriage with Elsa Pataky Chris Hemsworth opened up about the one rule that keeps his marriage to Elsa Pataky strong, and why her honesty matters more than praise.

K-drama Exclusive: Park Min-young, Wi Ha-jun ready for Bollywood on one condition Siren's Kiss showcases the nuanced acting talents of Park Min-young and Wi Ha-jun, who bring their characters' keen observational skills to life with compelling chemistry. The duo also share their admiration for Indian cinema, with Min-young expressing excitement about a potential Bollywood debut inspired by the magic of Aladdin, in an exclusive chat with India Today.

Kabir Singh, Ranbir's Animal, Salman's Prem represent same men: Sooraj Barjatya Sooraj Barjatya said that Kabir Singh, Animal and Salman Khan's Prem are sides of the same coin. He reflected on the passionate love displayed by all these protagonists.

Nawazuddin Siddiqui explains why he said yes to Sohum Shah's Tumbbad sequel Actor Nawazuddin Siddiqui shared what made him say yes to Tumbbad 2. Headlined by actor Sohum Shah, the sequel to the period folk horror film is set to go on floors soon.

Suriya promises vintage form in Vishwanath and Sons, first-look poster out The official title and poster of director Venky Atluri's new film starring Suriya and Mamitha Baiju were unveiled, with a release date confirmed for July. Producer Naga Vamsi called the film the return of vintage Suriya.

Dhurandhar The Revenge: Reports on trailer release date and runtime go viral Dhurandhar: The Revenge, starring Ranveer Singh, is reportedly set to unveil its trailer around Holi ahead of its March 19, 2026 release.

Trump, Israel have overplayed their hand: Javed Akhtar on Middle East conflict Javed Akhtar commented on the recent US-Israel strikes on Iran, and implied that "China will lose her total credibility in the world." He also referred to US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu as "bullies."

People with biased minds ignored the point: The Kerala Story 2 producer Vipul Shah The Kerala Story 2 producer Vipul Shah says people who criticise his film and its controversial beef-feeding scene have missed the point the script is trying to make.

Ammy Virk's daughter, wife stranded in Dubai: I'm a strong girl Papa, don't worry Punjabi singer and actor Ammy Virk opened up about fears for his family in Dubai. His wife and six-year-old daughter are in the United Arab Emirates amid rising tension. He expressed concern for their safety in an Instagram post.

Actor Hussain Dalal opens up on acting, writing and insecurity Hussain Dalal discusses juggling his acting and writing careers amid industry scepticism. His experience working with Vishal Bhardwaj on O'Romeo marks a significant milestone in his journey.

Rashmika and Vijay's chemistry is unmissable in new videos from puja ceremony Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda participated in Sathyanarayana puja at their new home in Thummanpet, Telangana. The couple got married on February 26 in Udaipur.

Jab Khuli Kitaab trailer: Pankaj Kapur, Dimple Kapadia and secret that tests love The trailer of Dimple Kapadia and Pankaj Kapur's upcoming film Jab Khuli Kitaab is out. The movie, scheduled for a March 6 premiere, will be available on Zee5.

general news.headline: Flights from Abu Dhabi land in India as airlines begin limited ops amid conflictAir travel in the Middle East is restarting cautiously after disruptions caused by the US-Israeli attack on Iran. India's civil aviation ministry said carriers are gradually restoring long-haul operations through alternative routings that avoid restricted Middle East airspace. Aircraft and crew repositioning is under way to stabilise schedules.

Live: Trump vows to continue Iran ops, refuses to rule out boots on the groundUS-Israel's Iran Strikes Live Updates: President Donald Trump on Monday claimed that Iran ignored repeated US warnings not to rebuild its nuclear program, warning that Tehran's ambitions could have soon posed a direct threat to America. The president was referring to US strikes on three Iranian nuclear sites in June, which the White House said "totally" destroyed Tehran's nuclear capabilities. Trump added that the regime already possessed missiles capable of reaching Europe and US bases abroad, and "would soon have had missiles capable of reaching our beautiful America." Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates:

Netanyahu appears in public hours after Iran's claim of strike on his officeIran's Revolutionary Guards said the targeted locations were "severely hit" during the tenth phase of retaliatory operations. Iranian state media claimed Benjamin Netanyahu's fate was "uncertain." Israel has issued no confirmation of any hit on the Prime Minister's office.
Attacked by Iran, Arab nations insist on joining offensive: TrumpTrump on Monday said that Iran's actions against Arab nations were "probably the biggest surprise" of the conflict so far.

Why Arab countries haven't attacked Iran despite being hitIranian missiles are hitting Gulf cities after the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. But Arab nations have not retaliated. But why have the Arab countries, which have wealth and weapons, chosen restraint? Is the Muslim Ummah a factor?

US jets downed in friendly fire, Israel bombs Lebanon as Middle East war widensThe Middle East conflict continued to widen on its third consecutive day with rising casualties and fresh regional flashpoints.  The United States, however, insisted that the ongoing military campaign against Iran would remain limited and not turn into a prolonged war.

US consulate shut, visa services halted after violent protests in PakistanThe US mission in Pakistan said the Consulate General in Peshawar suspended operations from 2 March, while appointments at the Karachi and Lahore consulates were cancelled due to ongoing demonstrations, road blockages and security restrictions around US offices.

Apple launches budget iPhone 17e, price starts at Rs 64,900Apple has launched the iPhone 17e, the most affordable member of the iPhone 17 series, in India today. The iPhone 17e starts at Rs 64,900 in India and will go on sale starting March 11.

Mohammad Amir is not wrong. India are yet to show up as a teamSanju Samson's unbeaten 97 rescued India from T20 World Cup elimination against West Indies, sparing careers. Yet, batting woes, Bumrah-dependent bowling, and butterfingers fielding expose deep cracks heading into the England semi-final.

Will US put boots on the ground in Iran? Trump official won't rule it outUS Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Monday that there are currently no American forces deployed inside Iran, but he deliberately refused to rule out the possibility as the conflict escalates.
Girl, 3, stuffed into cupboard as punishment at Kolkata preschoolA three-and-a-half-year-old girl was left severely traumatised after her preschool teacher forced her into a cupboard as punishment.

This isn't Iraq: US says Iran op not an endless war or for regime changeUS Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth rejects comparisons between the Iran assault and Iraq war. He stresses the campaign targets Tehran's military and nuclear power with surgical strikes, avoiding an endless conflict.

Why is Iran not an Arab country? Persian vs Arab identity explainedIran is often grouped with Arab nations, but it is not an Arab country. Its people are largely Persian, its language is Farsi, and its history stretches back to ancient empires long before Islam. Here's a clear guide to Persian and Arab identity.

West Indies, Zimbabwe stranded in Kolkata due to Middle East conflictThe West Indies cricket team are stranded in Kolkata after their T20 World Cup exit. The development comes due to airspace closures caused by tensions between Israel and Iran in the Middle East.

Khamenei's wife dies two days after him, was injured in US-Israel strikesKhamenei along with several members of his family, including his daughter, son-in-law and granddaughter, were killed in the US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Khamenei's wife also succumbed to the injuries incurred during the attack, as per media reports.

Dhurandhar The Revenge: Reports on trailer release date and runtime go viralDhurandhar: The Revenge, starring Ranveer Singh, is reportedly set to unveil its trailer around Holi ahead of its March 19, 2026 release.

Scores killed, Middle East burning, but Trump isn't calling it a war. Here's whyHundreds have been killed, and several countries are facing attacks since US President Donald Trump declared the start of major combat operations against Iran on Saturday. Why is Trump not calling it a war? And more importantly, is Trump's military action illegal as per American laws?

Apple launches iPad Air with M4 chip, price starts at Rs 64,900Apple has launched the iPad Air with M4 chip in India today. The M4 iPad Air starts at Rs 64,900 in India and will go on sale starting March 11.

Indian crew member killed after drone boat hits oil tanker off Oman coastAn unmanned drone boat struck oil tanker MKD VYOM off Oman's coast, killing one Indian crew member and triggering a fire before all 21 personnel were evacuated safely.

Snow likely in Jammu and Kashmir on Tuesday, humidity to veil SouthIndia will face a warm Tuesday with temperatures four to six degrees Celsius above normal in northwest regions. The IMD predicts light snow for Jammu and Kashmir as a western disturbance arrives.

The royal Holi ritual influencers are reviving in Jaipur and beyondGulal gota is a 400-year-old tradition once favoured by the Jaipur royals for playing Holi. Today, that same royal custom has become bigger than ever, turning gulal gota into one of the most sought-after picks of the festival this year.

Is UAE a country? Who are the Gulf nations? Middle East geography explainedAmid rising tensions involving Iran, Israel and the United States, many readers are confused about basic Middle East geography. Is the UAE a single country or a collection of states? Which nations are referred to as "Gulf states"? This explainer clearly breaks down the UAE's structure, the GCC members, and where Iran and Israel fit geographically.

FIFA World Cup 2026: Iran's participation uncertain amid ongoing military conflictIran's football federation president remains doubtful whether the national team can fulfil its World Cup fixtures in the United States after U.S.-Israeli strikes heightened regional tensions.

What caused huge tanker jam in Strait of Hormuz? See satellite imagesA digital fog of war has descended on the Strait of Hormuz. GPS jamming and signal spoofing are creating a massive tanker jam on the world's busiest oil route following recent military escalations.

sports.headline: Wounded Barcelona ready to make 'impossible, possible' against Atletico Madrid Barcelona face a daunting 0-4 deficit in the Copa del Rey semi-final second leg against Atletico Madrid. Hansi Flick has called upon his team to make the impossible, possible.

West Indies, Zimbabwe stranded in Kolkata due to Middle East conflict The West Indies cricket team are stranded in Kolkata after their T20 World Cup exit. The development comes due to airspace closures caused by tensions between Israel and Iran in the Middle East.

Captain? Won't even keep him in the team: Afridi lashes out at Pakistan player Former Pakistan captain Shahid Afridi remains furious at the team. He has lashed out at one of the players and said that, let alone captaincy, he does not even deserve a place in the team.

Is Sehwag unprofessional? Fans call out ex-batter over viral Miller remarks Former India batter Virender Sehwag's comment about David Miller has gone viral on the internet. Many cricket fans are calling Sehwag unprofessional due to the nature of his admission.

The IND vs PAK handshake row: What do the outsiders think ft. Bavuma, Vaughan Temba Bavuma says the India–Pakistan no-handshake gesture does not look good for cricket, while admitting he is not fully aware of the political context behind the decision.

Mohammad Amir is not wrong. India are yet to show up as a team Sanju Samson's unbeaten 97 rescued India from T20 World Cup elimination against West Indies, sparing careers. Yet, batting woes, Bumrah-dependent bowling, and butterfingers fielding expose deep cracks heading into the England semi-final.

Enjoyed the cupcake ad: South Africa coach takes dig at World Cup promo South Africa head coach Shukri Conrad has revealed that the team was well aware of the cupcake advertisement made by T20 World Cup broadcaster Star Sports. Conrad took a sly dig at the ad ahead of their semi-final clash against New Zealand.

FIFA chief calls for red cards to players covering mouths after Prestianni racism row FIFA president Gianni Infantino says players who cover their mouths while confronting opponents could face red cards if linked to racist abuse after the Vinicius Junior incident.

FIFA World Cup 2026: Iran's participation uncertain amid ongoing military conflict Iran's football federation president remains doubtful whether the national team can fulfil its World Cup fixtures in the United States after U.S.-Israeli strikes heightened regional tensions.

The night Sanju Samson fulfilled his prophecy IND vs WI, T20 World Cup: Sanju Samson played the best innings of his career to guide India to the semi-final of the T20 World Cup 2026. Samson, who debuted for India in 2015, finally fulfilled his prophecy.

Pakistan board fines players Rs 16 lakh each after T20 World Cup failure: Report Pakistan Cricket Board has reportedly imposed a Rs 16 lakh fine on each player following their early exit from the 2026 T20 World Cup. Pakistan failed to go past the Super 8 stage in the tournament.

Justice by Samson: Irfan Pathan says wicketkeeper reminded everyone of KohliT20 World Cup: Irfan Pathan said that Sanju Samson reminded everyone of Virat Kohli with his knock in the India vs West Indies clash on Sunday, March 1. Samson's knock helped India reach the semi-finals of the T20 World Cup.

Rohit Sharma rates 2007 final as his favourite India-Pakistan clashIndia's great Rohit Sharma relived some of his fondest memories from past T20 World Cup clashes against Pakistan, reflecting on a rivalry that has defined the tournament over the years.

Sanju Samson should be a regular in India's white-ball setup: Sourav GangulyT20 World Cup: Sourav Ganguly heaped praise on Sanju Samson for his match-winning knock against the West Indies on Sunday, March 1. Samson was the star of the show as India won the match by five wickets.

After talks with Zaheer Khan, BCCI approaches Harbhajan Singh for off-spinners campBCCI has approached former India greats Zaheer Khan and Harbhajan Singh for specialised camps at the Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru. VVS Laxman initiated talks to help groom the next generation.

You will get your chance: Rohit's message to Samson before T20 WC goes viralT20 World Cup: Rohit Sharma's message to Sanju Samson ahead of the T20 World Cup 2026 goes viral after the wicketkeeper helped India defeat West Indies in Kolkata. Samson's knock helped India reach the semi-final of the tournament.

Stranded in Dubai, Sindhu set to miss All England. Lakshya to lead India's campaignTwo-time Olympic medallist PV Sindhu is unlikely to participate in the prestigious All England Open Super 1000 tournament, which begins on Tuesday. The Indian shuttler has been stranded at Dubai airport amid escalating conflict in the Middle East following US-Israel strikes on Iran.

Prague Chess: D Gukesh implodes, loses to Abdusattorov to remain winlessGukesh's nightmare in Prague continued as time trouble cost him a winning position against Abdusattorov. The World Champion blundered twice in the endgame to suffer his fifth straight setback.

The smell of burnt rubber, the deafening roar: Watching an F1 car live hits differentYou can follow F1 for years. But nothing prepares you for the moment an F1 car fires up metres away, and the sound sends shivers down your spine.

Spain vs Argentina Finalissima in Qatar at risk after US, Israel attacks on IranEscalating tensions in the Middle East have cast uncertainty over the highly anticipated Finalissima between Spain and Argentina, originally set for March 27 in Doha. The Qatar Football Association's suspension of all tournaments, along with delays to other regional matches, has led organisers to reconsider the feasibility of hosting the event amid recent air strikes from US and Israel on Iran.

India to host Afghanistan for one Test and a three-match ODI series in June 2026The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) on Monday confirmed that Afghanistan are set to visit India in June 2026 to play a standalone Test and a three-match One-Day International (ODI) series.

Sanju Samson's Kolkata blitz on par with Kohli's MCG knock vs PAK: SrikkanthT20 World Cup: Kris Srikkanth has claimed that Sanju Samson's innings in Kolkata is on par with the knock played by Virat Kohli at the MCG in the T20 World Cup 2022 against Pakistan. Samson's 97 helped India reach the semi-finals of the T20 World Cup 2026.

Switched off phone, stayed away from social media: Sanju on handling tough phasesIndia opener Sanju Samson switched off his phone and stayed away from social media to safeguard his self-belief during a lean patch, a phase that ended with a match-winning knock in the do-or-die T20 World Cup clash against the West Indies.

The batter who came in from the cold: Sanju Samson's night, Eden Gardens' legendT20 World Cup, India vs West Indies: Sanju Samson had long been the backup, the nearly man—the gifted cricketer forever waiting for his turn. On Sunday night at Eden Gardens, Samson made sure no one would ever doubt him again.

Rulayega kya pagle? Sanju Samson's hilarious reaction to Surya's praises: VideoT20 World Cup: Suryakumar Yadav's praises were so high for Sanju Samson that the Indian wicketkeeper had to step in and ask the Indian captain not to make him cry in hilarious fashion. Samson's sensational knock helped India make it to the semi-final of the T20 World Cup.

Not built in a day: The making of Jammu & Kashmir's Ranji Trophy triumphFor decades, Jammu & Kashmir flirted with promise without sustaining it. Their maiden Ranji Trophy title was no miracle, but the product of structural reform, cultural reset, and relentless discipline. It is proof that ambition, when engineered properly, can finally endure.

Daniil Medvedev stranded in Dubai with his family due to Middle East conflictFormer world No. 1 Daniil Medvedev, along with his family and team, was stranded in Dubai on Sunday, a day after winning the Dubai Duty Free Tennis Championships for his 23rd career ATP title.

Abhishek Sharma's lack of form: His head is muddled, says Anil KumbleT20 World Cup: Anil Kumble feels that Abhishek Sharma's lack of form in the T20 World Cup 2026 is down to a mental issue after the Indian opener failed in the must-win clash against the West Indies. Abhishek was dismissed for 10 off 11 balls in Kolkata.

T20 World Cup: Sunil Gavaskar, Ravi Shastri slam mid-match laser show in KolkataT20 World Cup, IND vs WI: Former India cricketers Sunil Gavaskar and Ravi Shastri condemned the laser show held during India's innings at Eden Gardens in Kolkata on Sunday night.

Samson played best knock of his life but India not playing good cricket: AmirICC T20 World Cup: After India proved Mohammad Amir wrong by storming into the semi-finals, the former Pakistan pacer has reignited debate, conceding that Sanju Samson produced the knock of his life, while insisting that India, as a unit, are still not playing good cricket.

At Eden Gardens, Sanju Samson showed the only race worth running is your ownT20 World Cup: He was overlooked, benched, and made to wait. But Sanju Samson never stopped believing. At Eden Gardens, he finally got to show the world exactly what that belief was worth.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
